summary: One missing automation blocks full L5 readiness
detail: Snapshot shows Agent Readiness L5 Autonomous overall 95% with only code-review automation missing among Product & Experimentation checks. Installing review automation is the single highest-leverage readiness close-out before treating the tree as fully L5.

summary: Feature modules exist but remain untracked—commit + memory first
detail: Untracked but present modules (dream.rs, factory.rs, qa.rs, readiness_v2.rs, review.rs, wiki.rs, automations.rs) plus matching docs/* and .aegis/ indicate a coherent product surface that is not yet on main. Nightly dream should treat commit hygiene and memory fill as first-class outcomes, not afterthoughts.

summary: Prefer memory, docs, tests, and skills over large refactors
detail: Project already has tests that execute, QA skill, CI workflows, missions history, learning metrics, and factory coverage files. Prefer extending these rails (docs, small tests, skill bodies, memory) over greenfield refactors when improving the agent loop.

summary: Dream output should primarily fill memory and small proposals
detail: Dream cycle assets (docs/dream.md, dream.rs, assets/dream-cycle.svg) and empty Project Memory sections imply the intended loop is: snapshot → consolidate stack/commands/gotchas/conventions → extract lessons → propose small improvements → update skills. Keep outputs durable and secret-free.
